reviews.review_msg: This class was very boring: each seminar had between 1 to 3 ten minute student presentations. Most students just read their presentations. The lecture portion, where Ryan actually showed his presence, was quite informative. So why make his students suffer through 10 to 30 minutes of non-informative student drizzle is beyond me.

Second course I've had with Ryan. He's a chill, laid back prof but has somewhat high expectations on his essays. Found the course itself interesting. He tries to be engaging and funny. Overall a pretty decent prof and I would take more classes with him.

Humble yet knowledgable prof. He was very enthusiastic in the course material (as it was his thesis topic) and it showed through his enjoyment and involvement. Approachable and friendly, excellent prof.

Maybe it's just me, but I was bored out of my mind. Many classmates skipped class on a regular basis. If you can stay awake, there is a lot to learn. He's a nice guy but lectures aren't all that engaging.
Epic win with this class, my fave. Teacher got me into it when I was into it already.

I'm so glad I took this course with Ryan.  He is very helpful and accessible if you have questions about essay topics and creates a relaxed atmosphere in lecture and tutorial.  He's funny too!  Overall, a great prof.

Ryan's field is not Romantic literature - it's Victorian literature, so it was a bit frustrating having an instructor who regularly professed not to have satisfactory answers to questions. In his favour, he is understanding, solicitous, and accessible. My problems are less with him and more with not having an instructor better suited to the course.
